task.name: Self-healing end-to-end evaluation conductor
task.objective: Drive and verify the ENTIRE tmux-cli flow end-to-end against itself in a disposable session, then SELF-HEAL the tooling when the flow breaks: discovery → roadmap/goal-generation → taskvisor implementation → a verifiably-running app (docs/architecture/e2e-evaluator-design.md §1). One cycle = PROVISION → BOOTSTRAP → DRIVE → WATCH → MEASURE → JUDGE → (PASS=stop | FAIL=TRIAGE→task-report) → REPORT → TEARDOWN, up to 10 cycles, resumable across a cleared context. This file is the CONDUCTOR SPINE: it owns the lifecycle endpoints — PROVISION (§5), BOOTSTRAP (§5), TEARDOWN (§10) — and leaves contiguous labelled SLOTs for the DRIVE/WATCH/MEASURE/JUDGE/TRIAGE loop (execute-3) and the STATE/REPORT/SUCCESS+RESUME steps (execute-4). It COMPOSES the real flow against a target session; it never reimplements discovery, generation, or the daemon.

input.note: $ARGUMENTS = the scenario name and/or `--project <path>`. Empty $ARGUMENTS → run the DEFAULT scenario (the canonical Symfony dashboard-login full-flow run, §1) in a fresh `/tmp/<scenario>-<UTCstamp>` dir. `--project <path>` pins the target dir instead of the default `/tmp` location.
A no-arg re-invocation also RESUMES an in-progress run (design §6) — the resume-state read is execute-4's slot in step 1b.

output.primary: Per-cycle: a run-state file (`.tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/<scenario>.state.json`) and a per-cycle report (`.tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/e2e-report-cycle-<n>.md`) — schema + write rules authored by execute-4 (design §6).

requirements.cli: `tmux-cli` CLI MUST be reachable on PATH (the orchestrator drives the target via the CLI + raw tmux, never via the tmux-cli MCP tools — design §3).
requirements.tool: `tmux` MUST be installed (send-keys / paste-buffer / load-buffer / pipe-pane / kill-session are the drive + transcript primitives).
`docker` (with `docker compose`) MUST be available — the full-flow scenario brings the target app up and TEARDOWN downs its stacks (§10).
`claude` MUST be installed — BOOTSTRAP launches it in the target session to execute the real flow (§5).
requirements.note: The tmux-cli MCP server is NOT required to DRIVE: MCP tools are frozen into a Claude at startup, so a mid-loop `make install` would not refresh them. The orchestrator uses the CLI + raw tmux (re-read from disk every call) so it survives target rebuilds without restarting (§3).

glossary.term: This running `/tmux:e2e-evaluator` session (in THIS repo). It drives the target with `tmux send-keys`/`paste-buffer`, observes the target's pipe-pane log, and FILES defects with `/tmux:task-report` (self-contained — the standalone `task-report` command is the whole reporting contract; no consumer pipeline or auto-install watcher is required to file). It may optionally poll task state with `/tmux:task-list`, but it never blocks the loop waiting on a fix and never builds/pulls itself (design §3).

A fresh disposable session created with `tmux-cli start` (detached) in `/tmp/<scenario>-<ts>`. It runs `claude`, executes the real flow (discovery → roadmap → taskvisor → running app), and reports progress back to the ORCHESTRATOR pane.

One PROVISION → BOOTSTRAP → DRIVE → WATCH → MEASURE → JUDGE → REPORT → TEARDOWN pass (design §2). Max 10. Pristine every cycle: a failed cycle's session AND its `/tmp` content are reaped before the next (§10).

Step 2 (this slice): resolve the target dir (`--project` or default `/tmp/<scenario>-<UTCstamp>`, `git init` if the flow needs a repo), start the detached target session by exact name, and — whenever `$DISPLAY` is set and a native terminal exists — MANDATORILY attach a native terminal for the human view and VERIFY a client landed (headless ONLY when there is genuinely no GUI: no `$DISPLAY` / no native terminal binary) — design §5.

Step 3 (this slice): launch `claude` in the target with the trust prompt pre-accepted and bypass-permissions mode on, export `TMUX_CLI_ORCHESTRATOR_PANE`, and start pipe-pane logging BEFORE any DRIVE (design §5).
Sending the scenario instruction (and later inputs) to the target — always two-step: text via `paste-buffer`/`send-keys -l` THEN a SEPARATE `Enter` (design §4). Owned by execute-3 (loop slot).

Step 11 (this slice): the ordered reap between cycles and at terminal stop — daemon stop → `docker compose down` → worktrees+branches → `kill-session` → `rm -rf` (design §10). A step's failure is logged but the reap CONTINUES.
`tmux pipe-pane -o -t <target-pane> 'cat >> <session>.log'` — the full target transcript of record. Assertions grep the LOG, not the screen (screen-scraping is timing-fragile, design §4).

The env var BOOTSTRAP exports into the target so the target's `tmux-cli notify-orchestrator "<msg>"` (the execute-1 primitive, design §4 / decision #1) resolves THIS orchestrator's pane and replies atomically (buffer + paste-buffer, then a separate Enter — escaping handled in Go).

The normalized {phase, failure reason, goal/area} tuple (design §7). Dedupes defects across cycles and drives the loop guard: the SAME signature failing twice after a fix → escalate to the human (don't thrash). Owned by execute-3 (TRIAGE slot).

`.tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/<scenario>.state.json` — the durable run-state (design §6): `{scenario, cycle, max_cycles, status, history[]}`. `cycle` = the NEXT cycle to run; `status` ∈ in-progress|passed|exhausted|escalated. Written atomically (temp + rename) by execute-4's STATE step (step 8) and read by RESUME (step 1b). A machine-written, git-excluded owned file — `.tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/` follows the AGENTS.md owned-dir convention (`.tmux-cli` is already in `.git/info/exclude`/`.gitignore`).

`.tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/e2e-report-cycle-<n>.md` — the per-cycle human report (design §6), one per completed cycle, with a FIXED section order: Driven Summary / Failure Point / Defect Signature / Filed Task / Timing Table / Verdict. Written by execute-4's REPORT step (step 9).

boundary.note: BOUNDARY — /tmux:e2e-evaluator is a tmux-cli SELF-TEST conductor (design §14 non-goal). It is embedded/installable like the rest BUT is NEVER shipped to drive end-user projects: it tests tmux-cli's own full flow against itself in disposable sessions. It is black-box real-instance E2E, NOT a replacement for `make test-all`, and it does NOT change the daemon's implement/investigate/validate cycle — it only OBSERVES and TIMES it.
boundary.vs: /tmux:feature and /tmux:director drive REAL work through the flow (a brownfield feature, a greenfield roadmap) inside the user's project. /tmux:e2e-evaluator drives a DISPOSABLE scenario against a throwaway target purely to verify and self-heal the tooling — it consumes those conductors as the thing-under-test, it is not one of them.

llm.mandate: DRIVE VIA CLI + RAW TMUX, NEVER THE tmux-cli MCP TOOLS. MCP tools are frozen into a Claude at startup; a mid-loop `make install` would not refresh them, so the orchestrator must use the `tmux-cli` CLI + `tmux send-keys`/`paste-buffer`/`load-buffer`/`pipe-pane` (re-read from disk every call) to survive target rebuilds without restarting (design §3).

TWO-STEP SEND, ALWAYS. Every DRIVE is text FIRST (`paste-buffer`/`send-keys -l "<text>"`, or `tmux load-buffer` + `paste-buffer` for multi-line/quoted input — no shell-escaping) THEN a SEPARATE `Enter` key. NEVER a combined send; the PoC failure mode was a forgotten/merged Enter (design §4).

CONFIG-THEN-RUN — SEED `~/.claude.json` TRUST BEFORE ANY CLAUDE LAUNCH. Because `tmux-cli start` auto-launches `claude`, pre-seed the target dir's `hasTrustDialogAccepted` + global `bypassPermissionsModeAccepted` in `~/.claude.json` BEFORE `start` — never cancel-then-reseed a trust dialog after the fact (step 2). The order is config, THEN run.

SLASH-COMMAND THEN PROMPT — NEVER BUNDLE. To invoke a slash command in the target, send the BARE `/command` as its own two-step send (let it load) FIRST, THEN the scenario prompt as a SEPARATE two-step send. A bundled `/command + prompt` in one submission is a drive defect (step 4).

NEVER `pkill -f <projectname>` IN TEARDOWN — it matches the killer's OWN command line and SIGTERMs the teardown mid-run (observed: exit 144, `rm` skipped). Reap by EXACT session name + tracked PIDs only (design §10).

PIPE-PANE BEFORE ANY DRIVE. Start the pipe-pane log in BOOTSTRAP the instant the pane exists and before the first DRIVE, with `-o` (append) so a re-attach never truncates — else early transcript is lost and assertions miss it (design §4).

PRISTINE EVERY CYCLE. A failed cycle's target session AND its `/tmp` content are reaped via TEARDOWN (§10) before the next cycle; never reuse a dirty target dir (PROVISION fails loud on a non-empty dir).

ATTACH THE HUMAN VIEW WHEN A GUI EXISTS. In PROVISION, whenever `$DISPLAY` is set AND a native terminal binary exists, you MUST attach a native terminal to the target session and VERIFY a client landed (`tmux list-clients`) before BOOTSTRAP — headless is permitted ONLY when there is genuinely no GUI (no `$DISPLAY` / no native terminal). Silently running headless with a GUI present hides progress from the operator and is a defect, not a shortcut (step 2).

COMPOSE, DON'T REIMPLEMENT. The orchestrator drives the REAL discovery/generation/daemon flow inside the target; it never re-derives discovery questions, goal enumeration, or the dispatch loop. Only a genuine tmux-cli defect (reproduces on a clean retry, §7) earns a `/tmux:task-report`; LLM variance and bad-input are retried/fixed, never filed.
SLOT DISCIPLINE. This slice authors ONLY steps 1, 1b, 2, 3, 11. Steps 4–10 are labelled SLOT comments owned by execute-3 (loop) and execute-4 (state/report); do NOT author or renumber them.

step.title: CLI precondition
step.action: Assert the `tmux-cli` CLI is reachable on PATH (e.g. `command -v tmux-cli` / `tmux-cli --version` exits 0) and that `tmux`, `docker`, and `claude` are present.

check.condition: tmux-cli CLI not reachable on PATH
check.action: Print exactly: ERROR: tmux-cli CLI required on PATH but not found — install it (make install) and retry.

step.note: The tmux-cli MCP server is intentionally NOT a precondition — driving is CLI-only by design (§3). MCP-not-required-to-drive is a property, not a gap.

step.title: Read project context + resume state
step.action: Read AGENTS.md (or CLAUDE.md if AGENTS.md is absent) from THIS repo root and internalize conventions, architecture, and constraints before driving any cycle. If neither exists, proceed without project context.

startup-note.title: No prerequisite gate — reporting is task-report-only (self-contained)
startup-note.rule: There is NO consumer-pipeline / auto-install-watcher prerequisite and NO soft-pause gate. Defect reporting is `/tmux:task-report` AND NOTHING MORE — the standalone `task-report` command is the entire reporting contract; it files the defect to the backend on its own (honoring api.enabled / DEDUP / RECURSION-guard) and needs no external consumer or rebuild watcher to function. The loop NEVER blocks waiting for a fix to land. Proceed straight to RESUME below — do not probe for, assert, or pause on any reporting prerequisite.

resume.title: RESUME — read/init STATE_FILE, decide continue | stop | exhausted (the §6 resume read paired with step 10's write)
resume.rule: RESUME is IDEMPOTENT — read → reconcile → atomic rewrite, modelled on task-list.xml:44-68 reconcile-on-every-invocation and :86-89 drain/re-check-on-each-run. A no-arg re-invocation after a cleared context continues the run; a re-invocation mid-cycle is safe (last-writer-wins via the step-8 temp + rename).

branch.action: Treat as a FRESH run — this is NOT an error. Initialize `{ "scenario": <scenario>, "cycle": 1, "max_cycles": 10, "status": "in-progress", "history": [] }` and write it atomically (step 8). Missing file = cycle 1, never a failure.
The run is already terminal (`passed` | `exhausted` | `escalated`) — STOP cleanly; do NOT enter the loop or redo cycles. Print the terminal status and the last REPORT_FILE path.
The self-heal budget is spent — set `status: "exhausted"`, write atomically (step 8), and ESCALATE to the human (the §6 max-cycle guard). Do NOT enter the loop.
CONTINUE at `cycle` — because `cycle` is the NEXT cycle to run, cycles 1..`cycle`-1 are already done and are NOT re-run. Hand to PROVISION (step 2) for this cycle.

step.title: PROVISION — fresh target dir + detached session + terminal discovery
step.action: RESOLVE the target dir: use `--project <path>` if given, else default to `/tmp/<scenario>-<UTCstamp>` (kept under `/tmp/`). `git init` the dir if the flow needs a repo (design §5).

check.condition: the resolved dir already exists AND is non-empty
check.action: FAIL LOUD — do NOT reuse a dirty dir (a half-reaped prior cycle). Abort the cycle and report; the next cycle picks a fresh stamped dir. (Pristine-every-cycle, §10.)

step.action: PRE-SEED THE CLAUDE TRUST CONFIG BEFORE ANY CLAUDE LAUNCH (config-then-run, never fight the trust window). `tmux-cli start` AUTO-LAUNCHES `claude` in window 0 — so the trust/bypass config MUST already be on disk BEFORE this `start`, or the auto-launched claude lands on the "Do you trust this folder?" dialog and the run looks broken. Patch `~/.claude.json` for the resolved target dir FIRST: set `projects["<target-dir>"].hasTrustDialogAccepted = true` (+ `hasCompletedProjectOnboarding: true`) and the top-level `bypassPermissionsModeAccepted = true`, written atomically (temp + rename). Only AFTER the config is seeded do you run `tmux-cli start`. Never cancel-then-reseed a trust dialog after the fact — the order is config, THEN run (the operator-corrected ordering).

Start the TARGET session DETACHED via `tmux-cli start` bound to an EXACT, recorded session name (this exact name is the only safe kill target in TEARDOWN — never a pattern). The detached session works headless: send-keys/capture-pane/pipe-pane all operate without an attached terminal (design §3).

check.condition: `tmux-cli start` errors after its own retries
check.action: Abort this cycle and report the start failure (a harness/infra defect — see <error-reporting>). Do not proceed to BOOTSTRAP on a session that did not come up.

step.action: TERMINAL DISCOVERY — ATTACH A HUMAN VIEW (decision #4). The human view is a DELIVERABLE the operator asked for, NOT an optional probe you may skip: whenever `$DISPLAY` is set AND a native terminal binary exists, you MUST attach one to the target session and VERIFY a client landed before BOOTSTRAP. Do NOT silently fall through to headless when a GUI is available — that is the exact defect that hides progress from the human. Procedure, in order: 1. Gate: read `$DISPLAY`. If empty/unset → headless (the genuine CI/SSH path, step's branch below). Otherwise continue — attaching is now MANDATORY, not best-effort. 2. Resolve a NATIVE terminal binary in fixed order (first found wins): `konsole`, `gnome-terminal`, `xterm`, `alacritty`, `kitty`, `wezterm` — via `command -v <t>` resolving to a real host path (e.g. `/usr/bin/konsole`). EXCLUDE the Flatpak Konsole: NEVER launch via `flatpak run …`; a flatpak-only install is sandboxed (no host TTY) and is a trap — treat it as "not found". 3. Attach detached-safe with a concrete, backgrounded invocation that survives this orchestrator turn — e.g. `setsid <native-term> -e tmux attach-session -t <exact-session> >/dev/null 2>&1 &` (Konsole: add `--separate` so it never reuses an existing window). Attaching a SECOND client to the already-running detached session is safe and does not disturb driving (send-keys/capture/pipe-pane are unaffected). 4. VERIFY the attach: after ~2–3s, `tmux list-clients -t <exact-session>` MUST show ≥1 client. If zero, retry the launch ONCE (e.g. wrong/zombie binary), then re-verify.

branch.condition: `$DISPLAY` is set, a native terminal exists, but no client attaches after one retry
branch.action: Degrade to headless to keep the run alive, but ANNOUNCE it LOUDLY — print a WARN naming the terminal binary tried and the verify failure so the human knows WHY they cannot see progress (a missing human view with a GUI present is a reportable harness defect, not a silent fallback). Continue the cycle headless.

branch.condition: `$DISPLAY` is unset OR no native terminal binary exists
branch.action: Run FULLY HEADLESS (no attach) and CONTINUE — this is the genuine CI/SSH path and is NOT an error. Driving a detached session is unaffected. (This branch is reachable ONLY when there is no GUI to attach to — never as a shortcut around step 3 above.)

step.title: BOOTSTRAP — launch claude + inject orchestrator pane + start pipe-pane
step.action: Resolve THIS orchestrator's own pane id and export it into the target launch environment as `TMUX_CLI_ORCHESTRATOR_PANE` — the target's `tmux-cli notify-orchestrator "<msg>"` (execute-1 primitive) reads it to reply to this pane atomically (design §4 / decision #1).

Launch `claude` in the TARGET session with the trust prompt PRE-ACCEPTED (via the step-2 `~/.claude.json` pre-seed — do NOT rely on driving the dialog) and bypass-permissions mode ON, so respawns are non-interactive. Because `tmux-cli start` already auto-launched a plain `claude`, drop it to the shell (Esc/`C-c`) and relaunch `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` — which, with the seeded trust + `bypassPermissionsModeAccepted`, comes up straight at the idle `❯` prompt with NO trust dialog and NO bypass-acceptance screen. IGNORE the "N MCP servers need authentication" warning — only the `tmux-cli` MCP server matters to the target's flow (design §5 / decision #5).

Start the PIPE-PANE LOG IMMEDIATELY once the target pane exists and BEFORE any DRIVE: `tmux pipe-pane -o -t <target-pane> 'cat >> <session>.log'`. Use `-o` (append) so a re-attach never truncates. This log is the transcript of record — assertions grep it, not the screen (design §4).

check.action: ABORT the cycle (no transcript = no reliable assertions) and report. Do not DRIVE into an untranscribed session.

step.action: Wait for the target to be DRIVE-ready before handing off to the loop: poll the log/pane until the idle `❯` prompt is stable and no spinner (Cogitating/Germinating…) is running (design §4).

step.title: DRIVE — self-healing loop body + two-step send
step.rule: SELF-HEALING LOOP CONTROL. Steps 4–7 are the cycle body; one CYCLE = PROVISION (step 2) → BOOTSTRAP (step 3) → DRIVE (4) → WATCH (5) → MEASURE (6) → JUDGE/TRIAGE (7) → REPORT (execute-4 steps 8–9) → TEARDOWN (step 11). Run AT MOST 10 cycles, PRISTINE each (TEARDOWN reaps the prior target before the next PROVISION). Maintain a cycle counter in run-state (execute-4's state.json).

check.condition: the cycle counter would exceed 10 (an 11th cycle)
check.action: Do NOT start another cycle. Set `status: exhausted` in run-state and ESCALATE to the human (the self-heal budget is spent — repeated failures need a person). STOP the loop. This is the loop guard; a max-10-cycle run never silently spins.

step.action: Confirm DRIVE-readiness FIRST (carried from BOOTSTRAP step 3 and re-checked before every send): poll the pipe-pane log / `capture-pane` until the idle `❯` prompt is stable and no spinner (Cogitating/Germinating…) is running. Never send into a busy prompt (design §4).

TWO-STEP SEND, ALWAYS. Send the scenario instruction (and every later input) in TWO separate tmux calls: (1) the TEXT via `tmux load-buffer` + `tmux paste-buffer -t <target-pane>` (multi-line/quoted input — no shell-escaping), or `tmux send-keys -l` for a short single line; THEN (2) a SEPARATE `tmux send-keys -t <target-pane> Enter`. NEVER merge the text and the Enter — the PoC failure mode was a forgotten/merged Enter (design §4).

SLASH-COMMAND THEN PROMPT — NEVER BUNDLE THEM (the operator-corrected protocol). When the drive invokes a slash command (e.g. `/tmux:project-discovery`), do NOT send `/command <long prompt>` as one submission — the target must read/load the command's own instructions FIRST. Instead: (A) send the BARE `/command` alone as its own two-step send (text THEN a separate Enter) and wait for it to load/acknowledge; THEN (B) send the scenario prompt as a SEPARATE two-step send (text THEN a separate Enter). Two distinct submissions, each its own two-step. A bundled `/command + prompt` is a drive defect, not a shortcut. Drive the REAL flow (discovery → roadmap-generation → preflight gate (step 4b) → taskvisor → running app); never re-derive discovery questions or the goal enumeration.
After the instruction lands, hand control to WATCH (step 5) — do NOT poll the live channel; the target reports milestones via `tmux-cli notify-orchestrator`.

step.title: Preflight — HARD GATE between roadmap-generated and taskvisor-start
step.rule: This gate runs ONCE per cycle, AFTER the target's roadmap/`goals.yaml` is generated and elaborated, and BEFORE the target is driven to `taskvisor-start`. Real parallelism needs ALL of: the cap flipped, a parallelizable DAG, disjoint scopes, and host capacity (design §13/§14.1). A FAILED preflight BLOCKS `taskvisor-start` for this cycle (do not DRIVE the start) — the roadmap must be corrected (a planner/elaborator defect, not a worker one) before goals are consumed.

action.title: Set the cap (runtime override, no restart)

step.action: Write `max_goals=3` via the `.tmux-cli/taskvisor-concurrency` runtime override (`ConcurrencyOverridePath()`, concurrency.go:28) — NOT `setting.yaml` (which would need a daemon restart). N=3 is the conservative start for one dev host; step 5 below may raise it to 4 ONLY after host capacity is confirmed.
Run plan-audit over the generated `goals.yaml` using `DetectOverSerialized` (depinfer.go:329) — do NOT hand-roll a DAG check.

check.condition: DetectOverSerialized returns a non-nil SerializationFinding (near-linear / fan-out≤1 / runnable≤1)
check.action: FAIL preflight and BLOCK `taskvisor-start`. The roadmap is over-serialized (no concurrency possible); the produce/consume `depends_on` must be fixed before start. Route as a planner/elaborator defect via TRIAGE (step 7), not a worker retry.

step.action: Assert the about-to-run-concurrently goals declare disjoint file `Scope` using `DisjointReadySet(maxGoals)` (scope_gate.go:162) — the same gate the scheduler uses in slot-fill (statemachine.go:160).

check.condition: DisjointReadySet would serialize the ready set (overlapping OR unknown scope)
check.action: FAIL preflight and BLOCK `taskvisor-start`, FLAGGING the offending goal(s) whose Scope overlaps or is unknown (unknown scope = silently serialized). Fix the roadmap scopes before start.

step.action: Confirm goals are sized to ~20 min of work each (the 20-min/goal target is achieved by SIZING + parallelism, not by a bigger ceiling — design §14.3). Oversized goals are a roadmap-correction signal, not a ceiling bump.

Verify the host can run N concurrent docker compose stacks + worktrees (CPU/RAM) with NO host-port collisions (stacks are network-internal by default per the stack convention). Only if capacity is confirmed may N be raised to 4; otherwise keep 3.

action.title: Set the ceilings as soft guardrails

step.action: Set `max_wall_clock_sec` ∈ [1800, 2700] (a generous 30–45 min safety net) — NEVER a tight 30-min-minus value that would kill legitimately-borderline goals; enforced softly. Keep `progress_timeout_sec` ≈ 300 (config.go:93) so a wedged goal dies in ~5 min instead of eating the whole ceiling, and so it matches the WATCH silence window (step 5).

step.title: WATCH — event-driven primary, capture-pane fallback on silence
step.rule: EVENT-DRIVEN, NEVER POLL A LIVE CHANNEL. The PRIMARY signal is the target reporting milestones via `tmux-cli notify-orchestrator "<msg>"` (execute-1 primitive) into THIS orchestrator's pane (`TMUX_CLI_ORCHESTRATOR_PANE`, injected at BOOTSTRAP). Idle and react to those reports — do not `sleep`-poll or screen-scrape while the channel is live (design §4).

branch.action: Record the event, RESET the silence timer, and advance WATCH to the next expected milestone (discovery-done → roadmap-generated → preflight-passed → goals-dispatched → goals-done → app-up). The reset is what keeps a healthy long-running phase from tripping the fallback.

timeout.rule: Fire the FALLBACK only on (a) 300s of heartbeat-SILENCE (no `notify-orchestrator` line) OR (b) ANY phase breaching its §9 hard ceiling — whichever first. 300s ties to `ProgressTimeoutSec` (config.go:93) so the orchestrator wakes to diagnose at the SAME cadence the daemon would kill a wedged window — no premature false-trigger, no missed hang. The ceiling breach is the second, independent trigger (regardless of the silence timer).
timeout.action: FALLBACK DIAGNOSIS (read-only): `grep` the PIPE-PANE LOG and run `tmux capture-pane -p -t <target-pane>` to read the target's current state. If the log shows real progress (the channel just went quiet) → treat as variance, resume WATCH. If wedged (no progress, error, hung spinner) → hand to JUDGE/TRIAGE (step 7) as a candidate hang.

step.action: Continue WATCHing until the cycle reaches a terminal milestone (app-up = candidate PASS) or a fallback diagnoses a wedge (candidate FAIL) — then proceed to MEASURE (step 6) and JUDGE/TRIAGE (step 7).

step.title: MEASURE — per-phase timings + achieved parallelism (soft guardrails)
step.action: Record start/end timestamps for EVERY goal and EVERY phase (`elaborate`, `implement`, `investigate`, `validate`) — taskvisor already drives these transitions; read them from goal state + the pipe-pane log, do not re-instrument the daemon (design §9).

Across cycles, compute per-phase p50 / p90 / p95. Set the WARN threshold at observed p90 and the hard ceiling at `max(absolute §9 ceiling, p95 × 1.5)`. Flag any goal/phase over WARN (investigate) or over the ceiling (treat as a hang → fallback diagnosis (step 5) → TRIAGE (step 7)).

Record the MEAN in-flight goals per tick (achieved parallelism) vs `max_goals`. Achieved-parallelism ≪ N at `max_goals>1` is ITSELF a defect signal — the DAG/scopes serialized; route it as a planner/elaborator defect (over-serialized `depends_on` or non-disjoint Scope), NOT a worker retry (design §14.3).
step.rule: Timing thresholds are SOFT guardrails tuned from observed data — they feed reports + defect signals, they do not hard-kill goals (the daemon's `max_wall_clock_sec`/`progress_timeout_sec` own enforcement).

step.title: JUDGE + TRIAGE — verify end-state, file defects only on reproduce
step.action: JUDGE against the §8 end-state (full-flow scenario): discovery produced `docs/architecture/*`; a roadmap `goals.yaml` was generated; taskvisor drove all goals to `done`; AND the app is actually up — `GET /login` → 200, dashboard route unauthenticated → 302/401, authenticated request → 200 (a green daemon with a dead app is a FALSE pass). Combine with the MEASURE verdict: GREEN requires the build passing AND p90 within target (design §8/§13).

branch.action: Stop the loop: hand to execute-4's SUCCESS step (step 10). No TRIAGE, no task-report.

branch.tag: FAIL — end-state unmet or a phase wedged/over-ceiling
branch.action: Enter TRIAGE. Classify the failure with the defect-vs-variance-vs-bad-input rule below; only a tmux-cli DEFECT earns a `/tmux:task-report` (design §7).

table.title: Failure triage (decision authority: orchestrator, gated by reproduce-on-clean-retry)
table.row: reproduces on a clean PRISTINE retry with the SAME DEFECT SIGNATURE; failure is in the TOOLING (dispatch / worktree / compose / elaboration / daemon / planner-over-serialization), not the worker's code choices → FILE `/tmux:task-report`.
does NOT reproduce on a clean retry (a one-off bad worker choice / model noise) → RECORD as variance, retry the cycle, NEVER file a task-report.
the scenario/instruction itself was malformed → FIX the input, retry, NEVER file a task-report.

step.rule: DEFECT SIGNATURE = the normalized {phase, reason-class, area} tuple — lowercase and STRIP volatile goal-instance ids/timestamps (goal-007 vs goal-008, run stamps) so the SAME defect dedupes across cycles. This signature powers both DEDUP and the loop guard below.
step.action: On a confirmed defect (reproduced on clean retry, same signature): invoke the `/tmux:task-report` skill (DEDUP key = category+title+goal_id; `proposed_fix` non-stub; honoring api.enabled / RECURSION-guard) — do NOT re-implement reporting here (see task-report.xml). That is the WHOLE reporting obligation: filing the defect via `task-report` and nothing more. Record the filed task id, then TEARDOWN (step 11) → next cycle. Do NOT block the loop waiting for the task to be `resolved` or for a fix commit / rebuild; the orchestrator only FILES and continues (it never runs `git pull`/`make install`). A later cycle naturally re-tests against whatever binary is on disk (CLI re-read every call); if a fix has landed the signature clears, if not it dedupes.

check.condition: the SAME defect signature recurs across cycles (already filed once, still failing)
check.action: ESCALATE to the human and stop thrashing — do NOT re-file the same task (it is already filed + deduped). Same-signature-recurrence is the convergence loop guard (design §7).

step.title: STATE — write/update the durable run-state atomically (§6)
step.rule: STATE_FILE = `.tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/<scenario>.state.json` is the durable cycle ledger that the self-healing loop (step 4 — its `cycle` counter and `status: exhausted` write live HERE) and RESUME (step 1b) both read. Schema (§6): `{ "scenario", "cycle", "max_cycles": 10, "status", "history": [ {cycle, outcome, defect_signature, task_reported, task_status, git_after, app_up, durations} ] }`; `status` ∈ `in-progress` | `passed` | `exhausted` | `escalated`.

ATOMIC WRITE ONLY — write to a `<scenario>.state.json.tmp` temp file then `rename(2)` over the target (temp + rename, last-writer-wins), modelled on task-list.xml's atomic ledger rewrite (:44-68). NEVER truncate-in-place: a concurrent re-invocation must never observe a half-written file (resume idempotency; TestState_AtomicRewrite).

`cycle` SEMANTICS — `cycle` is the NEXT cycle to run, NOT the last completed. Bump `cycle` ONLY AFTER the REPORT step (9) has written its report; the just-finished cycle's `history[]` entry carries `cycle = cycle-1` (post-bump). Never off-by-one.
step.action: On a FAILED cycle: append the `history[]` entry (`cycle`, `outcome:"failed"`, `defect_signature`, `task_reported`, `task_status`, `git_after`, `app_up`, `durations` from MEASURE step 6) and bump `cycle += 1`; keep `status: "in-progress"`.
On the max-cycle guard (step 4 loop guard / RESUME): set `status: "exhausted"` (the exact value execute-3's loop guard and RESUME both write) and do NOT bump `cycle` past `max_cycles`.
On a PASSED cycle (step 10 SUCCESS): set `status: "passed"`, append the final `history[]` entry (with `app_up:true`), and do NOT bump `cycle` — the loop stops.

step.title: REPORT — write the per-cycle markdown report, then append history + rewrite state (§6)
step.rule: Post-cycle (after JUDGE/TRIAGE, step 7), write REPORT_FILE = `.tmux-cli/e2e-evaluator/e2e-report-cycle-<n>.md` with this EXACT, FIXED section order — every section present every cycle:
step.section: what the cycle drove: scenario, target dir/session, and the phases reached (discovery → roadmap → preflight → taskvisor → app-up).
the phase + milestone where the cycle failed (or "none — passed"); for a wedge, the silence/§9-ceiling trigger that fired (step 5).
the normalized {phase, reason-class, area} tuple (step 7) with volatile goal-instance ids/timestamps stripped — or "none" on PASS / variance / bad-input.
the `/tmux:task-report` task id + its current `/tmux:task-list` status (e.g. `task-281` / `resolved`) — or "none filed (variance / bad-input / pass)".

per-phase p90 (elaborate / implement / investigate / validate) AND the mean in-flight goals (achieved parallelism), BOTH from execute-3's MEASURE (step 6, §9/§13). A p90 over target, or mean in-flight ≪ `max_goals`, is flagged here as a perf/over-serialization signal.
PASS | FAIL | EXHAUSTED, with the one-line reason. PASS REQUIRES `app_up:true` (step 10 false-pass guard) — never declare PASS on "all goals done" alone.
step.action: After writing REPORT_FILE, append the cycle's `history[]` entry and rewrite STATE_FILE atomically (step 8). Report-THEN-state ordering keeps the report on disk BEFORE the `cycle` bump points the next resume past it (no report-less gap on a mid-write crash).

step.title: SUCCESS-CRITERIA + green-stop — the §8 end-state JUDGE (step 7) consults; the app-up probe is the gate
step.rule: SUCCESS-CRITERIA (consulted by execute-3's JUDGE, step 7). FULL-FLOW PASS requires ALL FOUR, ANDed: (1) discovery docs present (`docs/architecture/*`); (2) a roadmap `goals.yaml` was generated; (3) taskvisor drove ALL goals to `done`; (4) the app is VERIFIABLY UP (the app-up probe below).
step.action: APP-UP PROBE (the gate): `docker compose up` the target stack, then assert `GET /login` → 200, the dashboard route UNAUTHENTICATED → 302/401, and an AUTHENTICATED request → 200. Record the boolean as `app_up` in the cycle's `history[]` entry (step 8).
step.rule: FALSE-PASS GUARD — JUDGE may NOT return PASS while `app_up:false`. "All goals done" alone is NEVER a pass: a green daemon with a dead app is a false pass (design §8 deliverable-pinning). If every goal is `done` but the app-up probe fails, the verdict is FAIL with `app_up:false` → route to TRIAGE (step 7). The app-up probe — not goal status — is the gate.
step.action: SINGLE-COMMAND secondary mode: when the thing-under-test is a single command (not the full flow), derive the intended end-state from THAT command's own skill/XML `description`/contract and assert the concrete artifacts it CLAIMS to produce (design §8 secondary).

branch.tag: PASS — all four criteria green AND app_up:true AND p90 within target (§13)
branch.action: Set `status: "passed"` (step 8), write the final report (step 9), and STOP the loop — no further cycles, no TRIAGE, no task-report.

branch.tag: not green — any criterion unmet, or app_up:false, or a phase over §9 ceiling
branch.action: Verdict FAIL → JUDGE hands to TRIAGE (step 7). If this was already the max cycle, the RESUME / step-4 loop guard sets `status: "exhausted"` and escalates instead of re-cycling.

step.title: TEARDOWN — ordered reap (subroutine the loop calls between cycles and at terminal stop)
step.rule: Reap ALL artifacts the run spawned OUTSIDE `/tmp` (docker compose stacks, git worktrees, a taskvisor daemon) IN ORDER — stop consumers before removing what they hold. A step's failure is LOGGED but the reap CONTINUES to the next step (never abort the reap on a single failure). Mirrors the per-worktree compose-stack ordering fix: down stacks before removing the worktrees that hold them (design §10).
step.action: Stop the target's taskvisor/`tmux-cli` daemon for the target (graceful stop by the target's own handle).
`docker compose down` EVERY stack the run created (network-internal by default per the stack convention, but still must be downed or they leak + collide on host ports across cycles).
Remove the run's git worktrees and their `taskvisor/<goal-id>` branches.
`tmux kill-session -t <target-session>` by EXACT recorded name.
`rm -rf /tmp/<scenario>-<ts>` (the disposable target dir).
step.rule: NEVER `pkill -f <projectname>` (or any cmdline pattern that matches THIS teardown) — it SIGTERMs the teardown itself (exit 144, `rm` skipped). Target by EXACT session name + tracked PIDs only (design §10).

execution-rules.rule: DRIVE VIA CLI + RAW TMUX, NEVER the tmux-cli MCP tools: `tmux-cli` CLI + `tmux send-keys`/`paste-buffer`/`load-buffer`/`pipe-pane`/`kill-session` are re-read from disk per call and survive a mid-loop `make install`; MCP tools are frozen at the target's Claude startup (design §3).
TWO-STEP SEND ALWAYS: text (`paste-buffer`/`send-keys -l`, or `load-buffer`+`paste-buffer` for multi-line/quoted) THEN a SEPARATE `Enter` — never combined (design §4).
NEVER `pkill -f <projectname>` in TEARDOWN — reap by exact session name + tracked PIDs only; a cmdline-pattern kill SIGTERMs the teardown (design §10).
PIPE-PANE in BOOTSTRAP before any DRIVE, `-o` (append); the log is the transcript of record — assertions grep the log, not the screen (design §4).
Self-test ONLY: never ship/point this at an end-user project; it drives disposable targets to verify + self-heal tmux-cli's own flow (design §14).
execution-rules.error-reporting: On a genuine harness/infra/spec defect (an MCP tool or `tmux-cli` command that errored after its own retries, a contradictory/under-specified goal spec, or a validator-error-class condition), follow the shared `error-reporting` procedure in `tmux/task-report.xml` — compose + submit autonomously, honoring api.enabled / DEDUP / RECURSION-guard. NEVER on expected work failures (red validate cycle, supervisor pushback, NEED_INPUT, unwritten-code test red) or daemon-owned goal-failures.

llm.critical-rules: • Drive the target via the tmux-cli CLI + raw tmux (send-keys/paste-buffer/load-buffer/pipe-pane) — NEVER the tmux-cli MCP tools (frozen at startup; CLI survives mid-loop rebuilds, design §3) • Two-step send always — text THEN a separate Enter; large/quoted input via load-buffer+paste-buffer (design §4) • Config-then-run — seed ~/.claude.json trust (target hasTrustDialogAccepted + global bypassPermissionsModeAccepted) BEFORE tmux-cli start auto-launches claude; never fight the trust dialog after the fact (step 2) • Slash-command then prompt — send the bare /command alone (let it load) FIRST, then the prompt as a separate two-step send; never bundle /command + prompt (step 4) • TEARDOWN reaps in order (daemon → compose down → worktrees+branches → kill-session → rm -rf), continues past a single failure, and NEVER pkill -f <projectname> (design §10) • BOOTSTRAP injects TMUX_CLI_ORCHESTRATOR_PANE (the execute-1 notify-orchestrator contract) and starts pipe-pane BEFORE any DRIVE; PROVISION fails loud on a dirty dir (pristine every cycle) • PROVISION MUST attach a native terminal for the human view and VERIFY a client landed whenever $DISPLAY + a native terminal exist (Konsole first; exclude Flatpak) — headless ONLY with no GUI; never skip silently (step 2) • This slice authors ONLY steps 1/1b/2/3/11 — steps 4–10 are execute-3 (loop) and execute-4 (state/report) SLOTs; do not author or renumber them • Self-test conductor only — never ship to drive end-user projects; only a defect that reproduces on a clean retry earns a task-report (design §7/§14)
